PR surface: Source +142, Tests +454, Docs +5, Generated 0. Total +601 across 29 files.

target.title: feat(whatsapp): support opt-in group-name config
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #91174.

- Adds WhatsApp group config matching by exact current group name, gated behind `channels.whatsapp.dangerouslyAllowGroupNameMatching` at root or account scope.
- Keeps stable group JIDs as the preferred and highest-precedence key for group allowlists, require-mention overrides, tool policy, reactions, and group system prompts.
- Registers the new break-glass flag in schema/types, generated channel config metadata, security dangerous-flag collection, and docs.

reviewMetrics: added 1 WhatsApp config flag (`dangerouslyAllowGroupNameMatching`) at root/account scope; this matters before merge because mutable group-name matching affects group authorization and must remain explicit, audited break-glass behavior rather than an implicit fallback.

Docs:
- https://docs.openclaw.ai/channels/whatsapp
- https://docs.openclaw.ai/channels/groups
- https://docs.openclaw.ai/gateway/security

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/whatsapp/src/group-policy.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/group-gating.allowlist-warn.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/group-activation.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/system-prompt.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/accounts.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/web-auto-reply-monitor.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/ack-reaction.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/status-reaction.test.ts src/config/group-policy.test.ts src/config/zod-schema.providers-whatsapp.test.ts src/security/dangerous-config-flags.test.ts`
- `node --import tsx scripts/generate-bundled-channel-config-metadata.ts --write`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base live-openclaw/main --parallel-tests "node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/whatsapp/src/group-policy.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/group-gating.allowlist-warn.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/group-activation.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/system-prompt.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/accounts.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/web-auto-reply-monitor.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/ack-reaction.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/status-reaction.test.ts src/config/group-policy.test.ts src/config/zod-schema.providers-whatsapp.test.ts src/security/dangerous-config-flags.test.ts"` -> clean, no accepted/actionable findings

`pnpm docs:list` was not run because `pnpm` is not installed on this checkout's PATH.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: WhatsApp group config can now target an exact current group name when the dangerous opt-in is enabled, while stable group JIDs remain preferred and safe by default.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout using the production WhatsApp group-policy/system-prompt resolvers and security dangerous-flag collector through `node --import tsx`; no mocked test runner path.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx --eval '<probe imports extensions/whatsapp/src/group-policy.ts, extensions/whatsapp/src/system-prompt.ts, and src/security/dangerous-config-flags-core.ts; then resolves a config with groups["123@g.us"] and groups["Family Chat"]>'`

Evidence after fix: Terminal output from the production-code probe:

```json
{
  "nameAllowed": true,
  "nameRequireMention": false,
  "nameToolAllow": [
    "calendar.read"
  ],
  "namePrompt": "name prompt",
  "jidWinsRequireMention": true,
  "jidPrompt": "jid prompt",
  "defaultSafeNameAllowed": false,
  "dangerousFlags": [
    "channels.whatsapp.dangerouslyAllowGroupNameMatching=true"
  ]
}
```

Observed result after fix: Exact group name `Family Chat` resolved the configured group allowlist, mention policy, tool policy, and system prompt only when `dangerouslyAllowGroupNameMatching` was enabled; stable JID `123@g.us` took precedence when both JID and name matched; the same name key was rejected when the dangerous opt-in was absent; and the enabled break-glass flag appeared in dangerous config flag output.

What was not tested: Live WhatsApp group delivery in a real WhatsApp Web session; full repo `pnpm` docs/check lanes because `pnpm` is unavailable locally.
